articles.title: HEAR FROM ANTIREZ HIMSELF AT REDIS RELEASED
articles.summary: REDIS FOUNDER SALVATORE SANFILIPPO is virtually joining CEO ROWAN TROLLOPE to kick off Redis Released in San Francisco on Sept. 4 [4]—with a live keynote on how context engineering is shaping the agentic era.

articles.summary: Not in SF? JOIN REDIS RELEASED [5] IN A CITY NEAR YOU.

articles.title: MARK ZUCKERBERG SHAKES UP META'S A.I. EFFORTS, AGAIN
articles.summary: Meta is splitting its AI division into four groups. One will focus on AI research, one on 'superintelligence', another on product, and one on infrastructure. The reorganization is likely to be the final one for some time. It is aimed at better organizing the company so it can get to its goal of superintelligence and develop AI products more quickly to compete with others.

articles.title: AIRPODS PRO 2 SEEM SET FOR ANOTHER SURPRISE UPGRADE AT IPHONE 17 EVENT
articles.summary: Apple looks to be set to debut a big software upgrade for AirPods Pro 2 at its iPhone 17 event. The upgrade will implement Live Translation, which should enable real-time conversations with people who speak other languages. The arrival of the AirPods Pro 3 is taking longer than expected.

articles.title: BILL GATES-BACKED AI COMPETITION OFFERS $1M TO ACCELERATE ALZHEIMER'S RESEARCH
articles.summary: Bill Gates and the Alzheimer's Disease Data Initiative have launched a global competition that aims to accelerate Alzheimer's disease research using AI. The winning AI tool will be publicly available for researchers worldwide. AI has the potential to revolutionize the pace and scale of dementia research. Gates announced the initiative in November 2020, just months after his father died from the disease at age 94.

articles.title: GOOGLE, KAIROS POWER PLAN ADVANCED NUCLEAR PLANT FOR TENNESSEE VALLEY AUTHORITY GRID BY 2030
articles.summary: Google and Kairos Power plan to deploy an advanced nuclear power plant connected to the Tennessee Valley Authority's (TVA) electric grid by 2030. The TVA has agreed to purchase up to 50 megawatts of power from the reactor. Kairos' Hermes 2 reactor will help power Google's data centers in Montgomery County, Tennessee, and Jackson County, Alabama. Kairos and Google will bear the financial risk associated with building the project, while the TVA will provide the revenue stream the plant needs to operate through the power purchase agreement.

articles.title: RECREATIONALLY OVERENGINEERING MY LOCATION HISTORY
articles.summary: This developer wanted a map that showed places they have already been in a visually pleasing way. They also wanted a way to share live locations. This post discusses how they built a tool to do just that.

articles.title: TWICK
articles.summary: Twick is an AI-powered video editing toolkit. It features a canvas timeline, drag-and-drop editing, AI captions, and serverless MP4 export. Twick contains a collection of packages for individual and image manipulation built with modern web technologies. It is perfect for building custom video apps.

articles.title: GENAI FOMO HAS SPURRED BUSINESSES TO LIGHT NEARLY $40 BILLION ON FIRE
articles.summary: US companies have invested between $35 to $40 billion in generative AI initiatives, and so far have almost nothing to show for it. Only 5% of organizations have successfully integrated AI tools into production at scale. Confidence in AI initiatives is declining among corporate leaders. The divide is due to the inability of AI systems to retain data, adapt, and learn over time, rather than insufficient infrastructure, learning, or talent.

articles.title: SAM ALTMAN ON GPT-6: ‘PEOPLE WANT MEMORY'
articles.summary: Sam Altman told reporters in San Francisco last week that GPT-6 will arrive faster than the gap between GPT-4 and GPT-5. The model will adapt to users, allowing people to create chatbots that mirror personal tastes. Altman says he sees memory as the key to making ChatGPT truly personal. OpenAI has been working closely with psychologists to help shape the product.

articles.title: CRAVING MORE AI IN YOUR INBOX?
articles.summary: TLDR AI is your daily fix of LLMs, GenAI, and deep learning goodness. Same TLDR format. Still free.

articles.title: HOW LOW CAN COLO GO, ASKS JLL, AS DATACENTER VACANCY RATES NEAR ZERO
articles.summary: Co-location capacity in North American datacenters has dropped to a record low.

articles.title: THE GREAT SSL CERTIFICATE PANIC
articles.summary: New SSL/TLS certificates will expire every 47 days by 2029 - companies that adopt early will gain competitive advantages through more robust, automated infrastructure.

articles.title: COMPUTER SCIENCE GRADUATES FACE WORST JOB MARKET IN DECADES
articles.summary: Computer science graduates are facing 6.1% unemployment in 2025 - computing engineering majors are faring even worse at 7.5% unemployment.

articles.title: WHY DO VIDEO GAMES USE KERNEL-MODE ANTI-CHEATS?
WYOMING'S 'FRONTIER' STABLECOIN DEBUTS ON ETHEREUM, SOLANA, AND AVALANCHE
articles.summary: Wyoming's stablecoin, issued in partnership with LayerZero, will operate across Ethereum, Solana, and Avalanche, along with Ethereum scaling networks Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism, and Base.

articles.title: ANALYSIS OF THE GFW'S UNCONDITIONAL PORT 443 BLOCK ON AUGUST 20, 2025
articles.summary: The Great Firewall of China exhibited anomalous behavior between approximately 00:34 and 01:48 Beijing Time on August 20 that caused massive disruption of the Internet connections between China and the rest of the world.
